item.title: Thursday Fresh Meme Dump for your Coffee #281
item.description:
1. Just why?
2
3. Stupid dumb society
4. What if this actually happened to you and you actually don't remember?
4
6
7
8. Homemade food > Fast food (except for KFC)
9
10
11. Is this part of the exercise?
12. Guys, I really need some privacy here Dogs:
13. Big Unites
14
15. Thank you for making it to the end of this dump, I hope you were thoroughly entertained! I would like to share with you that I'm making a wacky game called Do Not Press The Button, and it's humor and style are kind of inspired by the Stanley Parable. I'm working on tons of fun stuff to do in it! For example: JOINING THE CHAIR REVOLUTION! If you liked the humor in this dump, you would probably like the game. Please wishlist: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ Thank you for supporting indie devs!

item.dc:creator: ThatGuy42

item.title: Margaret Atwood trying to burn an unbearable addition of Handmaids tale
item.description: Margaret Atwood, the 84-year-old Canadian novelist and poet, is pictured here attempting to burn an 'unburnable' copy of her novel The Handmaid's Tale with a flamethrower. A single unburnable copy was created to raise awareness about increasing censorship; her dystopian science fiction novel, which centers around one woman's quest for freedom in a totalitarian theocracy where women's rights are completely suppressed, has been the subject of numerous censorship challenges since its publication in 1985. The unburnable copy was auctioned off after her flamethrowing attempt, raising $130,000 for PEN America, a literary and free expression advocacy organization. As Atwood famously asserted in her poem ""Spelling"": ""A word after a word after a word is power.""

item.title: Incredibly Rare 13 In 100 Billion Event Seen At CERN Particle Accelerator
item.description: September 25, 2024 James Felton Senior Staff Writer Edited by Maddy Chapman Scientists at the European Organization for Nuclear Research (CERN) have observed an incredibly rare event, which could possibly hint at new physics beyond the Standard Model. There's nothing particle physicists like more than crashing particles together and seeing what comes out of the mess. Such experiments can help us find evidence of particles and processes predicted by the Standard Model of particle physics – such as the Higgs Boson – or sometimes hint that we are missing something, such as the decay of the B meson. “The Standard Model describes the fundamental forces and building blocks of the universe. It is a highly successful theory, but there are several mysteries of the universe that the Standard Model does not explain, such as the nature of dark matter and the origins of the matter–antimatter imbalance in the universe," Professor Mark Thomson, chair of the Science and Technology Facilities Council and contributor to the NA62 experiment, explained in 2020. “Physicists have been searching for theoretical extensions to the Standard Model. Measurements of ultra-rare processes provide an exciting avenue for exploring these possibilities, with the hope of discovering new physics beyond the Standard Model.” One ultra-rare event being probed by the NA62 experiment is the decay of a charged kaon into a charged pion and a neutrino and anti-neutrino pair. Kaons, also known as K mesons, are strange (and sometimes anti-strange) beasts. Protons and neutrons are usually made up of three quarks. For protons that's two up quarks and one down quark, for neutrons, it's one up quark and two down quarks. Kaons contain two quarks, combinations of up quarks and anti-up quarks, strange and anti-strange quarks. Charged kaons (K+) are made of an up quark and an anti-strange quark. They are of particular interest as their decay is very precisely predicted by the Standard Model. According to these predictions, fewer than 1 in 10 billion charged kaons will decay into a charged pion and a neutrino-antineutrino pair (K+ → π+νṽ). The NA62 experiment attempts to search for this decay by colliding a high-intensity proton beam into a stationary target, which then produces secondary particles that can be detected, measured, and identified (though neutrinos are identified through missing energy). In 2020, the team reported evidence of this rare form of decay being detected by the experiment. Now, after far more collisions, including higher-energy collisions, the team reports a 5-sigma detection, meaning there is a 0.00006 percent chance that the detection is a statistical fluke. "With this measurement, K+ → π+νṽ becomes the rarest decay established at discovery level – the famous 5 sigma," Cristina Lazzeroni, Professor in Particle Physics at the University of Birmingham, said in a statement. "This difficult analysis is the result of excellent teamwork, and I am extremely proud of this new result." While the decay is rare, as predicted by the Standard Model, it is around 50 percent higher than expected, occurring about 13 times in 100 billion. It is unclear what causes this discrepancy between the Standard Model's predictions and the results observed, with possible explanations including new particles or new physics, both of which are pretty exciting. "This is the culmination of a long project started more than a decade ago. Looking for effects in nature that have probabilities to happen of the order of 10-11 is fascinating and challenging," Professor Giuseppe Ruggiero, from the University of Florence, added. "After rigorous and painstaking work, we have got a stunning reward to our effort and delivered a long-awaited result." While the team has announced and presented results at CERN, full papers and further experiments will follow. The team presented their findings at a CERN EP seminar. Source: https://www.iflscience.com/incredibly-rare-13-in-100-billion-event-seen-at-cern-particle-accelerator-76091
item.dc:creator: thedoci

item.title: Dr. Ziegler - Mercy - Cosplay from Overwatch
item.description:
Okay, you all earned this - I guess I will show you Mercy :D You all did an amazing job of helping out with the Extra Life Charity Event last week, so I supposed you can see the full cosplay, as was mentioned in my video :D Reference Post here to get caught up: https://imgur.com/gallery/extra-life-lfg-lgvVpT2
In only a week we have raised $1,701 - Which is incredible!!! Can you help us push it over the finish line? Only $500 to go!! Donate here to help an amazing cause with me! https://www.extra-life.org/participant/521502
Reference Image of course! This was fun to put together after the @imgur Team suggested it to me :D
item.dc:creator: Alsoashley

item.title: The PowerPenz Doodler Pen!
item.description: There sure are a whole lotta wacky Etch A Sketch variants out there! If you're reading this description and happen to have the red variant of this toy, I'd love to chat about how I can take it off your hands :) Also yes, I intentionally dressed to match this glorious hunk of plastic lol

item.title: The 474th vol. of "I'm not actually removing these from my phone, I just wanted to share them so I could feel some sort of connection in this dismal world" Dumps - SPOOPY SEASON, Week 1
item.description:
mmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmm
STAY TUNED! I'll be releasing more MeeMays back into the wild *every other* Monday-Friday around 11:30 am, GMT-5, in my ""I'm not actually removing these from my phone, I just wanted to share them so that I could feel some sort of connection in this dismal world" Dumps" Or use Imgur's "Follow" Feature, which will alert you whenever one of my dumps goes Viral. If you wish to chat, I'm frequently in ElbowDeepInAHorse and PotatoCheesMonk's Discord Servers between the hours of 7pm and 1am GMT-5 during the week, and at unpredictable but frequent times Saturdays and Sundays
This is my newly commissioned Signature Image, by Dark Arts Studios. He's no longer on The Imgurs, I met him on ElbowDeepInAHorse's Discord Server. Here's where you can see his work: https://twitter.com/Anubis23342060 https://www.furaffinity.net/user/thed4rk1ord/ https://tailsofthegods.com/ https://dark-arts.itch.io/ FUN TIP / LIFE FACT / PRO HACK I upvote 90% - 99% of positive or neutral comments on my posts, as well as ≈50% of negative ones Shoutout to my fellow Dumpers: @DeafKitten @CodyBurkett @OhValleyOfPlenty @AbbiiStabbii @DigiT00l @MemeMuseumCurator @CeleryMan7 @DumpsterKoala @JebidiahBeetus @Kaeldra @KirbyVictorious @HermioneDanger00 @DumpReview @WhiskeyJelly @JasonPositivityPilot @danat101 @PotatoCheesMonk @VanessaNelson @NotMuchBiggerThanAWomprat @MindNumbingBraIncAndy @TheMellowMoron @TheWoodsAreVile @ThatOtherGirlYouKnow @IStealMemesAndStuff @DrWalters @DaymanAhAAAAah @SecretHarpy Lemme know If you are a Regular Dumper and would like to be added to the list! Archive Of Previous Dumps: Vols 1-300: https://imgur.com/a/vAfF9Wz Vols 301+ : https://imgur.com/a/oDakPaq -----FRESHLY UPDATED----- #Spooky #Spoopy #Memes #MeeMays #AdolfHitlerSchoolForTheArts #TheWhoring20s #SaveTheBEEEEEES #BeeLM #SaveTheBees #JettfreyEpsfuelCandnt #CommeltSteelBeamicide #WhatIfNobodyShotJFK #AndHisHeadJustDidThat #EatingAppsTakingNaps #EatNamesAndTakeAss #MasturbatingIsNecrophilia #IfYoureDeadInside #DumpyMcGeeAndGuyNamedSteve #2Dumps1Site #AllTiddiesAreGoodTiddies #FreeTheNipple #GregFromHR #SteveFromAccounting #DougFromSales

item.title: Sandra Bullock

item.title: Things are looking up. An overly friendly manatee looking through a clear kayak.
item.description: I've been through a lot of hurricanes over the years but this was the first one I've taken damage. At about 8 pm that night the water breached the banks of the intracoastal waterway at my house and started rushing inland. By 9pm there was about 2 feet of water rushing through the first floor. I saw mullet and sand eels swimming through the garage as I was wading around trying to salvage what I could from the rising water. I didn't lose anything irreplaceable, but probably have about $15,000 worth of damage to the house. I didn't lose any products, but lost about $7,000 worth of big boxes for shipping kayaks and paddleboards as well as a few other business supplies. Not complaining at all though. I'm grateful we didn't lose more. Some people lost everything. People lost their cars, as in they floated away and they couldn't find them. And there was so much sand on the main road (Gulf Boulevard) that when the plows and tractors finally cleared the road the sand was piled as high as 10 feet on both sides of the road. It reminded me of the day after the 2007 blizzard that hit Colorado, except with lots of damage. We've almost finished tearing out the drywall on the first floor already and with a lot of hard work and a few more very long days things should start getting back to normal and I can get back on the water.
item.dc:creator: SeeThroughCanoe

item.title: Images of Note: hell yes
item.description:
no
Fuck you nintendo, let me play your out of print games.
Love how capitalism literally is forcing us to use euphemisms.
You know if you ban people from verifying your claims, that to me is proof you're lying.
Donald Trump thinks he's "most people".
not gonna lie that just sounds like a horrible feature.
This is the correct response.
If you demand your children give you grandchildren against their will, you need to look at yourself in the fucking mirror.
this feels like a horrible place to live.
owo
they will!
two possibilities: 1. Their Peace 2. Their Shenanigans.
then they die, it's a very efficient system.
http://discord.abbii.gay: For Chats http://twitch.abbii.gay: For Stream http://tumblr.abbii.gay: For shitposts http://mastodon.abbii.gay: For microblogging

item.title: It's a me problem, I know
item.description: I'm not saying I'm smarter than anyone else, or whatever. I have high empathy, and I always always wonder about the two sides to a story, or third, or fourth, because I seek to understand people even if I don't agree with them. And the world is awful, man.
